cover_letter: Benjamin Cleek (847)707-6794 | ben.cleek@icloud.com | LinkedIn [Date:] [Hiring Team] [Hiring Company] Re: Application for [Position Title] Hi Hiring Team, I am a marketing student at Butler University, and when I learned about the opportunity with [Company Name], it immediately felt like the kind of environment where I could learn in a real and meaningful way. I am especially drawn to your work because [insert one specific detail about the company, recent project, brand positioning, or industry focus]. I have always been interested in brands that know who they are and communicate that clearly, which is why the automotive and motorsports space feels like the right fit for me. Growing up, this industry was always part of my world, and it is what sparked my interest in brand strategy. I am hoping to contribute to a team that values thoughtful work and steady progress, and your program stands out as a strong match. Much of what I have worked on so far has helped me understand how brands earn trust and loyalty. At Trulli Audio, I supported early stage marketing where everything was built from the ground up. I worked trade shows, helped with sales enablement materials, researched competitors and customer segments, and contributed to the first go to market strategy. I also completed a brand audit and strategy project for Jaguar Motors at Butler, where my team refreshed their positioning for younger consumers. These experiences showed me that I am at my best when I am researching, organizing insights, and helping shape the direction of a brand. I enjoy the process of understanding what makes customers care and translating that into clear strategic ideas. For Summer 2026, I am hoping to join a company that is steady, collaborative, and committed to thoughtful brand building. I would love to contribute to a team where I can bring curiosity, strong follow through, and a calm, steady approach to every project. I would value a chance to work in an environment that combines creativity with structure. I look forward to the opportunity to learn from your team and contribute to the work you are doing. Thank you for considering my application. Sincerely, Ben Cleek

job_label: Eiteljorg Museum Marketing and Communications Internship
job_description: Salary Range:  per hour. The Eiteljorg Museum of American Indians and Western Art in downtown Indianapolis seeks a motivated college student or recent graduate to assist the Marketing and Communications Department in its efforts to promote and raise awareness of the museum’s events, exhibits and programs during summer 2026. Through this paid marketing internship, a student can learn practical, valuable workplace skills that will bolster any resume. The summer internship would last from the end of May or early June 2026 until early August 2026, typically 20 to 25 hours per week during business hours on weekdays, with required evening and weekend events. Major responsibilities include assisting the Marketing and Communications staff with tactical creative execution of plans, uploading digital images to the museum website, maintaining daily presence on Instagram Facebook TikTok LinkedIn and others, updating online tourism and community calendars, taking photographs and shooting and editing video, writing calendar items news releases e-newsletter items and blog items, distributing printed materials, attending internal and external meetings, and other duties as assigned. Qualifications include major in public relations, digital marketing, journalism, advertising, communications, tourism or related field, experience with social media and social media marketing, Microsoft Office and Adobe Suite, some photography and video production and editing, and some experience with website builders and design. Internship is primarily in-person in Indianapolis, though some work may be done remotely with supervisor approval.

job_description: May 26 to August 1 2026, dates flexible, 25 to 30 hrs per week, paid. The Missner Group is seeking a motivated and adaptable Marketing and Communications Intern to join the team for Summer 2026. The intern reports directly to the Director of Marketing and Communications and works collaboratively to develop a customized plan aligned with individual goals while gaining direct exposure to company leadership and insight into marketing within a fully integrated real estate and construction firm. No prior experience required.

job_label: Business Process Analyst Global Services & Support
job_description: The Business Process Analyst for Global Services & Support is responsible for analyzing designing and optimizing end-to-end service and support processes to translate service strategy into coordinated cross-functional execution. The role acts as a bridge between Global Services & Support Finance Manufacturing and IT, ensuring operational needs are converted into scalable repeatable and well-governed systems. Responsibilities include owning end-to-end process design documentation and governance; identifying inefficiencies and friction points across workflows; facilitating alignment when tradeoffs arise; ensuring processes support internal controls audit readiness and operational rigor; partnering with stakeholders; serving as translator between business users and IT; owning intake and prioritization for system enhancements; translating business problems into functional requirements; collaborating with BizOps Insights on metrics; overseeing UAT; driving structured change management; establishing process governance standards; and ensuring documentation controls and ownership are defined. Qualifications include bachelor’s degree in business operations or related field, experience owning or materially contributing to enterprise-scale process design and/or system implementations, experience in scaled audit-aware or regulated environments, strong working knowledge of CRMs ERPs MRPs and related systems, ability to work through ambiguity and drive alignment, and exceptional communication organization and attention to detail.

job_label: Process Improvement Analyst II Process Excellence
job_description: Process Improvement Analyst II – Process Excellence. Candidates based in Florida are highly preferred OR fully remote open to EST and CST candidates. Required 2-4 years process analyst business process management project management and/or related experience. The role evaluates redesigns and enhances business processes across functional areas, supports operational excellence by applying process improvement methodologies, conducting research and analysis, and partnering with cross-functional teams to deliver meaningful measurable improvements. Responsibilities include process evaluation and design, applying Lean Six Sigma ISO Agile or related frameworks, data and root cause analysis, leading or supporting multiple improvement initiatives, facilitating working sessions with stakeholders, change management support, documentation and workflow development, performance monitoring, stakeholder consultation, training and presentations, quality and compliance focus, risk awareness, process integration, and maintaining awareness of industry processes trends and regulatory practices. Ideal qualifications include 2-4 years of process analysis business process management or project management experience, strong analytical and problem-solving skills, cross-functional collaboration, and insurance industry knowledge preferred.

cover_letter: January 15, 2023 The American Israel Public Affairs Committee 251 H St NW Washington, DC 20001 Dear Selection Committee, I am writing to express my interest in the AIPAC Diamond Summer Internship Program. Having recently attended the Political Student Leadership Summit, where I had the privilege of lobbying on Capitol Hill, I am eager to bring my communication skills and passion for political activism to AIPAC. My interest in political engagement derives from a belief in the ability of individuals to make a positive difference through advocacy. I am particularly drawn to AIPAC's commitment to strengthening the US-Israel relationship, and I am eager to contribute to the organization's mission. During the Political Student Leadership Summit, I strengthened my persuasive communication skills while advocating for critical legislation. Through engaging with elected officials with BUCKiPAC, I have improved my ability to grasp policy issues, engage with stakeholder groups, and manage the complexities of the political scene. In addition, during my year-long campus engagement internship with OSU Hillel, I developed creative strategies to engage effectively with new students across campus.
extra_context: Interested in finding something she will enjoy and grow from.
job_description: Policy Analyst - Human Resources Department. Under administrative direction, responsible for planning and administering a specific policy analysis in the Human Resources Department. Responsibilities include planning and administering projects or programs, supervising activities of personnel, monitoring budgets, designing and monitoring procedures utilized in program evaluation, developing commitments from departments and agencies, reviewing policies and procedures, preparing project status reports, preparing financial and narrative reports, communicating with municipal officials and public and private entities, and performing related duties. Essential functions include policy development, data analysis, compliance and research, advisement, and training. Qualifications include two years of equivalent education training and experience in program administration required, five or more years of related experience preferred, public sector experience preferred, SHRM or project management certification preferred, excellent communication, prioritization, organizational skills, attention to detail, time management, and Microsoft Office proficiency including SharePoint.

target_roles: Policy Analyst / Junior Policy Analyst; Regulatory Affairs Analyst; Legislative Assistant; Legislative Aide; Public Policy Associate; Government Affairs Assistant; Compliance Analyst; Client Advocacy Specialist; Public Interest Advocate; Legal Intake Specialist; Legal Services Coordinator; Case Analyst; Privacy Analyst; Data Protection Analyst; Information Governance Analyst; Cyber Privacy Consultant; Cybersecurity Policy Analyst; Program Associate (Legal Programs)

job_description: Entry Level Legal Assistant at Gebeloff Law Group in Boca Raton, Florida. The position teaches drafting basic legal documents, serving lawsuits, filing legal documents, communicating with clients, other law firms and the Court, and managing legal calendars. Good communication skills are required. Must be willing to work in a small office under supervision of attorneys and paralegals. Bilingual Spanish is a plus. After probationary period, paid time off and health insurance offered. Pay increases considered as skills advance. Small sized commercial litigation law firm with high volume of cases and room to grow.
